narration: You're scrolling your phone at 9PM and suddenly you've bought things you didn't plan on. That's not weak willpower — your brain is just out of gas.
visual: Person silhouette lying on couch holding a glowing orange phone, clock showing 9:00 PM, three shopping bags appearing one by one

narration: Scientists call it decision fatigue. Your brain makes over 200 decisions every day, and by 9PM your financial judgment is basically gone.
visual: Brain SVG outline with battery meter draining from full to nearly empty, decision counter ticking up to 200-plus, brain turning red when depleted

narration: Here's the dark part — retailers know this. They send their biggest deals between 8PM and midnight, right when your resistance is at its lowest.
visual: Shopping cart icon next to ringing notification bell, 24-hour time bar chart with 8PM-to-midnight columns lit up in orange, 'DEAL ENDS SOON' alert badge

narration: The average American racks up $2,100 in late-night impulse buys every year. Over a decade, that's $21,000 gone from evening scroll sessions.
visual: Piggy bank SVG with a crack forming, dollar counter animating up to $2,100, decade total badge of $21,000 springing in below

narration: Here's the fix — and it doesn't need willpower. Block shopping apps after 8PM. People who do this save an average of $1,800 a year without any budget.
visual: Phone SVG with red X over a shopping cart icon, gold lock symbol springing in, green '$1,800 SAVED' badge and checkmark appearing

narration: You don't need more discipline — you need a smarter schedule. Delete shopping apps from your home screen tonight, and follow for more money psychology.
visual: Person silhouette standing upright with orange glow, stack of gold coins building up beside them, orange 'FOLLOW FOR MORE' CTA badge
